name: Plantain Fufu
intro: Made using unripe green plantain, this swallow is simple to make and is often eaten as a low carbohydrate alternative to Amala or Cassava Fufu.
method: Place the plantains in a blender along with the water and blend until you have a smooth mixture.

Pour this mixture into a pot and stir continuously with a wooden spoon over medium heat. After a few minutes, it will begin to thicken and change colour. You will know it is ready when it becomes sticky, smooth and turns a slightly brighter, almost translucent yellow colour.

Place the yam in a pot and cover completely with water. Cover and cook on medium-high heat for 25 minutes until the yam is very soft. Drain (reserve some of the water) and place the boiled yam in a food processor with half a cup of the reserved water. Blend until you have a smooth, sticky dough.
